skill.name: paid-ads
skill.description: Create paid advertising strategy, campaign structure, ad copy, targeting, testing plans, and optimization guidance for major ad platforms.
skill.summary: Plan and optimize paid advertising campaigns across Google, Meta, LinkedIn, X, and TikTok.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged markdown fences, advertising terminology, and planning language as dangerous code patterns. Review found no executable scripts, no network calls, no credential access, and no prompt injection in SKILL.md. The only residual concern is a low-risk wording issue where the skill claims direct ad-account access despite containing guidance only.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown code fences and plain advertising templates. They are documentation examples, not executable Ruby, shell, or generated commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside markdown fences and contain campaign naming or ad-copy placeholders. There is no executable interpreter context or user-input command construction.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match ordinary marketing words and headings, such as paid ads guidance and setup guides. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption library, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language in front matter or headings. I found no evidence of cryptographic code or credential handling in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are advertising terms, planning questions, and optimization checklist items. They do not collect host information, enumerate files, inspect processes, or query system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss ad platforms, job titles, bid strategy, and common marketing mistakes. No operating-system reconnaissance behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Overstated Platform Access Claim
low_findings.description: The skill says the assistant has direct access to ad platform accounts. The package only contains SKILL.md guidance and no integration code, so this is a low-risk accuracy issue rather than a confirmed security threat.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The access claim is explicit, but no supporting code or external tool instructions exist in the scanned file. The issue is misleading capability framing, not malicious execution.

content.user_title: Plan Paid Ad Campaigns Across Channels
content.value_statement: Paid ad teams need clear campaign plans before spending budget. This skill turns goals, audiences, offers, and metrics into structured paid media guidance.
actual_capabilities: Recommends paid advertising channels based on campaign goals, audience, budget, and offer type.
Creates campaign structure guidance for Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, X, and TikTok.
Provides ad copy frameworks, headline patterns, CTA options, and creative testing ideas.
Explains audience targeting options, including custom audiences, lookalikes, keywords, and job-based targeting.
Suggests optimization actions for CPA, ROAS, CTR, CPM, conversion rate, and ad fatigue.
Provides setup and audit checklists for tracking, pixels, tags, UTMs, audiences, and creative assets.
limitations: It does not connect to advertising platforms or change live campaigns directly.
It cannot verify current platform policies, prices, or interface changes without external research.
It depends on accurate business, audience, budget, and conversion data from the user.
It gives marketing guidance, not legal review for regulated advertising claims.

use_cases.title: Launch a New Paid Search Campaign
use_cases.description: Build a campaign plan with objectives, keyword themes, ad copy, budget allocation, tracking checks, and launch priorities.

use_cases.title: Improve Paid Social Performance
use_cases.description: Diagnose weak CTR, high CPA, ad fatigue, audience overlap, and creative testing gaps across social platforms.

use_cases.title: Prepare a B2B LinkedIn Plan
use_cases.description: Define target roles, company filters, lead gen forms, document ads, budget expectations, and conversion tracking needs.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Ad Platform
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a first campaign.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the best paid ad platform for this offer. My product is [product], target audience is [audience], goal is [goal], monthly budget is [budget], and landing page is [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Create Campaign Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a paid ads campaign structure for [platform]. Include campaign objective, ad sets or ad groups, targeting, budget split, naming convention, conversion events, and initial tests.

prompt_templates.title: Write Ad Copy Variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Write ad copy for [offer] using problem-agitate-solve, before-after-bridge, and social proof angles. Include primary text, headlines, CTAs, and creative notes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Optimize Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these paid ads results and recommend actions: spend [amount], impressions [number], clicks [number], CTR [rate], conversions [number], CPA [amount], ROAS [number], audience [details], and creative notes [details].

output_examples.input: A SaaS team wants more demo requests from marketing directors with a monthly budget of $8,000.
output: Primary channel: LinkedIn for role and company targeting, with Google Search for high-intent demo keywords.
Initial split: 60% LinkedIn lead gen, 30% Google Search, 10% retargeting.
First tests: pain-point ad copy, benchmark report offer, demo CTA, and company-size targeting.

output_examples.input: A Meta campaign has high CPM, low CTR, and no conversions after one week.
output: Likely issues: narrow audience, weak creative hook, or landing page mismatch.
Recommended actions: broaden targeting, refresh the first three seconds of video, test a clearer offer, and verify pixel events.
Decision rule: pause variants with enough spend and no signal, then shift budget to higher engagement creative.

output_examples.input: A brand needs search ad headlines for a project management product.
output: Outcome angle: Project Plans Your Team Will Use.
Efficiency angle: Save Hours on Project Updates.
Pain angle: Tired of Missed Deadlines?

best_practices: Provide objective, budget, offer, audience, landing page, and conversion data before asking for a plan.
Ask for platform-specific recommendations when you already know where the campaign will run.
Use the output as planning guidance, then verify platform policies and tracking inside the ad account.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to spend budget, publish ads, or access live accounts directly.
Do not optimize only for clicks when the business goal is leads, sales, or revenue.
Do not use the same creative and audience assumptions across every platform without testing.

faq.question: Does this skill manage live ad accounts?
faq.answer: No. It provides campaign planning, copy, targeting, audit, and optimization guidance. It does not connect to ad platforms.

faq.question: Which ad platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, X, TikTok, and general paid media planning concepts.

faq.question: Can it write ad copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes frameworks for primary text, headlines, CTAs, hooks, and creative testing angles.

faq.question: Can it help diagnose poor campaign performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review metrics such as CPA, ROAS, CTR, CPM, conversion rate, and audience performance.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the goal, budget, offer, audience, landing page, existing results, tracking status, and creative assets.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for platform policy review?
faq.answer: No. You should verify current ad policies, claim rules, and regulated industry requirements before launch.
